name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: Cash America Pawn Benbrook, Texas — FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, ...
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn store chain with over 3,300 retail locations across 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America (including Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is listed on both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating its scale and market position in the consumer lending sector.

FirstCash primarily serves cash and credit-constrained consumers through pawn loans—non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property. Customers can pawn items including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments to receive immediate cash. Beyond pawn lending, FirstCash operates retail merchandise sales from its stores, gold and precious metal buying services, and a layaway service (10% down payment required). The company also owns AFF, a subsidiary providing lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through a network of over 15,000 retail merchant partner locations.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive international footprint and scale—operating more locations than virtually any competitor in the pawn industry. The company serves both consumers seeking quick cash and those without access to traditional credit. Their diverse merchandise buying and selling operations create a secondary revenue stream beyond lending, and their established infrastructure allows for rapid in-store transactions and immediate cash payment upon item evaluation.

Pawn loans offer genuine advantages for consumers facing temporary cash shortages without credit checks or debt obligations, since loans are secured by collateral rather than creditworthiness. However, customers must be comfortable surrendering personal items for the loan period, and failure to repay results in loss of the collateral. Interest rates and fees on pawn loans can be substantial, and the company's business model inherently caters to financially vulnerable populations, raising questions about whether this lending method genuinely improves financial outcomes for repeat customers.
address: 8103 Camp Bowie W Blvd, Benbrook, TX 76116

company_info.headquarters: 8103 Camp Bowie W Blvd, Benbrook, TX 76116

pros: 3,300+ locations in 29 U.S. states plus international presence ensures accessibility for most consumers
No credit check required—pawn loans based on collateral value, not creditworthiness
Immediate cash payment on the spot after item evaluation
Non-recourse loans—defaulting results only in loss of collateral, not debt collection or credit reporting
Diverse merchandise buying—accepts jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sports equipment, and instruments
Gold and precious metal buying service for consumers with items to liquidate
Layaway service available (10% down) as alternative to borrowing for purchases
cons: Pawn loan interest rates and fees can be substantial compared to traditional personal loans
Customer must forfeit personal property for duration of loan, creating temporary loss of access
Limited to item-based lending—loan amounts capped by collateral value, not income or need
Retail merchandise inventory quality and selection varies significantly by location
Business model inherently targets financially vulnerable consumers, raising questions about sustainability of repeated borrowing
best_for: Consumers facing immediate cash needs who lack access to traditional credit or cannot wait for loan approval
Individuals with items of value (jewelry, electronics, instruments) they can temporarily part with
People who prefer collateral-based lending without credit checks or debt obligation
Those seeking to sell gold, jewelry, or other valuables for immediate cash payment
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing urgent cash needs who have items of value to pledge as collateral and either lack access to traditional credit or need faster funding than banks/lenders provide. The primary caveat is that pawn lending works best as a temporary solution for short-term cash gaps; repeated reliance on pawn loans to bridge chronic financial shortages indicates a need for deeper financial intervention or income-based lending products.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property
Retail merchandise sales (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, instruments)
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Item valuation and immediate cash payment
Lease-to-own payment solutions through AFF subsidiary network (15,000+ merchant partners)
Online store locator to find nearest location
